author: Robert Lucier
title: Block Island Yoga Intermediate
summary: Intermediate (more experience )
content:
Intermediate (more experience )
This class includes a full practice of warm ups, pranayama (breath work), postures (asana), longer holdings, and deep relaxation (shavasana). 

For those practitioners with a moderate to advanced level of experience.

Fees
​Drop–in     $15.00
8 Classes    $100.00

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Sound Baths at Spring Street Gallery
summary: Sound has an amazing affect on the human psyche. A sound bath is a meditative experience where you will be ‘bathed’ in ambient, relaxing sound waves.
content:
Sound has an amazing affect on the human psyche. A sound bath is a meditative experience where you will be ‘bathed’ in ambient, relaxing sound waves.   

All you have to do is relax and listen.

Yoga mats, blankets, pillows and silk eye covers will be provided.

Anyone is welcome to bring their own yoga mat or chair (or beanbag chair if you’re amazing).

Space is limited;  first come, first serve.

 

$25 for a 45 min sound bath. cash, check or venmo accepted

author: Austin Crawford
title: Farmers Market @ Historical Society
summary: This will be the last Farmers Market of the year. This Farmers Market will join the last Block Island Arts and Guild Fair. Locally grown fruits and vegetables, honey, shellfish, Photography, Pottery, Island made Arts and Crafts.
content: This will be the last Farmers Market of the year. This Farmers Market will join the last Block Island Arts and Guild Fair. Locally grown fruits and vegetables, honey, shellfish, Photography, Pottery, Island made Arts and Crafts.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Arts & Craft Guild at Block Island Historical Society
summary: This will be the last Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild of the year. This Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild will join the last Farmers Market.
content:
This will be the last Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild of the year. This Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild will join the last Farmers Market. 

 

There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here. 

 

If you’d like to take home an artistic reminder of beautiful Block Island, one of the best spots to browse and buy takes place outside of the Block Island Historical Society. From 9 am to 1 pm, the lawn becomes a showplace and marketplace for members of the Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild. Choose among paintings, photographs, sculpture, jewelry, fabric arts, candles, and more. All of the items are handmade by local artists and would make a perfect keepsake for yourself or a gift for the folks back home.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Washington Trust 225 Birthday Celebration
summary: Customer appreciation day August 22
content:
Customer appreciation day August 22

please join us to celebrate Washington Trust bank 225 birthday.

We are having a raffle with $150 in local gift cards. We have limited edition coasters and customer coloring books featuring fun bank history.

Blocksicle will be here giving out popsicles

author: Austin Crawford
title: Bake, Book and Bloom at Island Free Library
summary: Summer kicks off, at the Library, with the Bake, Book, and Bloom Sale, which will be held by the Friends of the Island Free Library on Saturday May 25th at 9 a.m. There will be the traditional offerings of baked goods, used books, and a front lawn filled with plants and flowers from Clark Farms.
content: Summer kicks off, at the Library, with the Bake, Book, and Bloom Sale, which will be held by the Friends of the Island Free Library on Saturday May 25th at 9 a.m. There will be the traditional offerings of baked goods, used books, and a front lawn filled with plants and flowers from Clark Farms.

content:
ServSafe Alcohol Classes (TIPS Certification) offered on Island by Rhode Island Hospitality Association - Sponsored by the Tourism Council (see flyer below).

Register by emailing Jessica@blockislandinfo.com

author: Austin Crawford
title: 2025 Block Island Triathlon
summary: The Island's largest and most popular race begins with a 1/4 mile open water ocean swim; followed by 12 miles of biking over rolling hills with breathtaking views of the island. The last stretch is a 4 mile run on beautiful Cresent Beach. Questions? Call Dave 401-466-3223. email: recreation@new-shoreham.com.
content:
The Island's largest and most popular race begins with a 1/4 mile open water ocean swim; followed by 12 miles of biking over rolling hills with breathtaking views of the island. The last stretch is a 4 mile run on beautiful Cresent Beach. Questions? Call Dave 401-466-3223. email: recreation@new-shoreham.com.

The race begins at 9:00 a.m. There is a 6:00am boat departing Point Judith race morning. If you aren't on the island Friday night, you will be required to take this boat. Please don't forget to buy a ticket for your bike. If you plan on being on island Friday night, make reservations now. If the ferry is canceled race day, or there is severe weather; the race will be canceled. Race fee is non-refundable. 

Roads are NOT closed during the bike portion.

https://newshorehamri.gov/148/Recreation

author: Austin Crawford
title: Easter Brunch and Egg Hunt at Avonlea
summary: We invite you to join us, whether you are solo, bring your family along, come with friends, or fur babies. All are welcome to join us and the event is made to be exciting for all ages. Free to our guests at Blue Dory and Avonlea. Open to public for $20 per person for brunch. Please call us at least a week ahead if you plan to join us so that we are prepared. Children under 12 eat for free.
content:
We invite you to join us, whether you are solo, bring your family along, come with friends, or fur babies. All are welcome to join us and the event is made to be exciting for all ages. Free to our guests at Blue Dory and Avonlea. Open to public for $20 per person for brunch. Please call us at least a week ahead if you plan to join us so that we are prepared. Children under 12 eat for free.

 

Easter Brunch Festivities include:

 

We start the night before, March 30, with coloring Easter eggs in the Blue Dory bistro. We provide eggs and coloring using a couple different techniques. We encourage you to share your unique talents with others.
Wake up Easter morning on Block Island where we celebrate with brunch! Coffee is available early, and then brunch is from 9-11 am. From decadent brunch classics to homemade pasties, our talented chef, Brian Rusin, has curated our best breakfast of the year. Delight in a sumptuous array of gourmet delights, including freshly baked pastries, savory omelets, fresh fruits and bottomless mimosas.
No Easter celebration would be complete without an Easter egg hunt for all the children. If the weather is cooperative, we hide eggs in the Blue Dory garden for the children to find. If not, we will still have it inside. No peeking before we start at noon!
Not bringing children with you? Don’t worry, there is a special treat for the adults also!
So bring the whole family, there is fun to be had for all!

Contact us today by calling 401-466-5891 or book online HERE to reserve your spot. We encourage you to stay multiple nights and take advantage of one of our Spring Specials.

author: Austin Crawford
title: James Stover Exploration - TBD
summary: Advance sign-up is encouraged but not required. Email: kim.gaffett@tnc.org.
content:
Advance sign-up is encouraged but not required. Email: kim.gaffett@tnc.org.

The James Stover Block Island Exploration Series

Discover the fascinating nature of Block Island. Guided monthly walks for families (with kids of all ages) and individuals. Explore areas and species special to Block Island.  These walks are funded by friends of James Stover (1996-2003), in memory of a boy who loved exploring Block Island.


Programs are weather dependent
address: Block Island New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Arts & Craft Guild at Block Island Historical Society
summary: There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here.
content:
There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here. 

If you’d like to take home an artistic reminder of beautiful Block Island, one of the best spots to browse and buy takes place outside of the Block Island Historical Society. Several times each summer, from 9 am to 1 pm, the lawn becomes a showplace and marketplace for members of the Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild. Choose among paintings, photographs, sculpture, jewelry, fabric arts, candles, and more. All of the items are handmade by local artists and would make a perfect keepsake for yourself or a gift for the folks back home.

This year, the dates are set for June 29, July 13, July 27, August 10, August 24, September 7, and October 11. There’s no charge for admission to the Fair.
